headline: Boston College stuns LSU; Ohio State flexes dominance
subhead: Week 6 saw the power poll's consensus challenged by a surprising upset and reinforced by one of the season's most decisive performances.
lead: Boston College's 38-28 victory over LSU stands as the week's most jarring result, upending a top-five team and raising hard questions about LSU's ability to sustain momentum in crucial moments. Elsewhere, Ohio State's 45-15 dismantling of USC sent an unmistakable message about the gap between the elite tier and the rest—a performance that may reshape perception of the playoff picture as we enter the season's second half.

segments.title: Boston College Ambushes LSU in Major Shocker
segments.body: LSU came into Week 6 ranked third in the power poll, yet Boston College's defense forced critical turnovers and held the Tigers to a pace they could not sustain. The coaching room's preparation was evident in how they schemed coverage and applied pressure in the second half, effectively neutralizing an offense that had been clicking. This is the kind of result that forces a top-five program to recalibrate its identity moving forward.

segments.title: Ohio State Puts USC on Notice Early
segments.body: Ohio State's 45-15 victory wasn't just a win—it was a demolition that exposed the gap between title contenders and the middle tier. The offense established dominance on the ground, controlled tempo, and USC's defense had no answer for the pace and execution. This kind of comprehensive victory, early in the week's slate, set the tone for how rankings conversations would shift by Sunday night.

segments.title: Penn State's Defense Stymies Colorado's Offense
segments.body: Colorado managed just 14 points against Penn State's relentless defensive front, which collapsed the pocket and kept the Buffaloes' attack off rhythm from start to finish. The secondary also limited explosive plays downfield, forcing the offense into obvious passing situations. It was the kind of suffocating performance that suggests Penn State's defensive unit is hitting its stride at the right time of the season.

segments.title: Tennessee Holds Top Spot Despite Texas Stumble
segments.body: Tennessee's 22-18 win over Texas tightened the race at the summit of the power poll. Texas remains ranked highly despite the narrow defeat, but Tennessee's ability to win close games—especially on the road in a hostile environment—reinforces why the staff has the Volunteers perched at number one. Both teams are clearly elite, but Tennessee's composure in tight moments continues to be the differentiator.

segments.title: Oklahoma's Resilience Puts Notre Dame on Edge
segments.body: Oklahoma's 34-31 upset of Notre Dame, ranked third in the power poll heading into the week, signals that the Sooners are quietly finding their rhythm when it matters. After being a consensus bubble team early in the season, Oklahoma's offense executed with precision down the stretch, and the staff's adjustments proved decisive in a one-possession environment. Notre Dame remains ranked, but this loss introduces doubt into what had felt like a coronation.

closer: Week 7 will test whether LSU can bounce back from the Boston College loss or whether cracks in that program are beginning to show, while Ohio State's next challenge will determine if their dominance over USC was a statement or an outlier.
